title: Docker Agent Configuration
description: Configuration schema for Docker Agent v7

providers.description: Map of custom provider configurations. Providers define reusable defaults (base_url, token_key, api_type) that models can reference.

mcps.description: Map of reusable MCP server definitions. Define MCP servers here and reference them by name from agent toolsets to avoid duplication.

permissions.description: Tool permission configuration for controlling tool approval behavior

audit.description: Audit trail configuration for governance and compliance. When enabled, all agent actions are recorded with cryptographic signatures for tamper-proof audit trails.

ProviderConfig.description: Configuration for a custom model provider. Can be used for custom gateways

api_type.description: The API schema type to use. Determines which API schema to use.

base_url.description: Base URL for the provider's API endpoint (required)

token_key.description: Environment variable name containing the API token. If not set, requests will be sent without authentication.

model.description: Model to use for this agent (can be just model name or provider/model format)

fallback.description: Fallback model configuration for automatic failover and retry behavior

welcome_message.description: Optional welcome message to display when the agent starts

toolsets.description: List of toolsets available to the agent

sub_agents.description: List of sub-agents. Can be names of agents defined in this config, external references (OCI images like 'namespace/repo' or URLs), or named external references using 'name:reference' syntax (e.g. 'reviewer:agentcatalog/review-pr'). External agents without an explicit name are named after their last path segment.

handoffs.description: List of agents this agent can hand off the conversation to. Can be names of agents defined in this config, external references (OCI images like 'namespace/repo' or URLs), or named external references using 'name:reference' syntax (e.g. 'reviewer:agentcatalog/review-pr'). External agents without an explicit name are named after their last path segment.

max_consecutive_tool_calls.description: Maximum consecutive identical tool calls before the agent is terminated. Prevents degenerate loops. 0 uses the default of 5.

num_history_items.description: Number of history items to keep

add_prompt_files.description: List of prompt files to add

commands.description: Named prompts for /commands. Supports simple string format or advanced object format with description and instruction.

oneOf.description: Simple command format: the string becomes the instruction
Simple command format: the string becomes the instruction

structured_output.description: Structured output configuration for constraining model responses to a specific JSON schema. Supported by OpenAI (native) and Google Gemini (native). Anthropic requires prompt engineering or tool-based approaches.

name.description: Name of the response format schema

description.description: Optional description of what the schema represents

strict.description: Enable strict schema adherence (OpenAI only). When true, all properties must be in required array.

schema.description: JSON Schema object defining the structure of the response. Must include type, properties, and required fields.

type.description: Schema type, must be 'object' for structured outputs

rag.description: List of RAG sources to use for this agent

add_description_parameter.description: Whether to add a 'description' parameter to tool calls, allowing the LLM to provide context about why it is calling a tool

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks for executing shell commands at various points in the agent's execution

skills.description: Enable skills discovery for this agent. When enabled, the agent can discover and load skill files (SKILL.md) from the workspace.

CommandConfig.description: Advanced command configuration with description and instruction

description.description: Description shown in completion dialogs and help text

instruction.description: The prompt sent to the agent. Supports bang commands (!`command`) and positional arguments ($1, $2, etc.)

FallbackConfig.description: Configuration for fallback model behavior when the primary model fails

models.description: List of fallback models to try in order if the primary model fails. Each entry can be a model name from the models section or an inline provider/model format (e.g., 'openai/gpt-4o').

retries.description: Number of retries per model with exponential backoff for retryable errors (5xx, timeouts). Use 0 or omit for default (2 retries = 3 total attempts per model). Use -1 to disable retries entirely (try each model only once).

cooldown.description: Duration to stick with a successful fallback model before retrying the primary. Only applies after a non-retryable error (e.g., 429 rate limit). Use Go duration format (e.g., '1m', '30s', '2m30s'). Default is '1m'.

HooksConfig.description: Lifecycle hooks configuration for an agent. Hooks allow running shell commands at various points in the agent's execution lifecycle.

pre_tool_use.description: Hooks that run before a tool is executed. Can allow/deny/modify tool calls.

post_tool_use.description: Hooks that run after a tool completes. Can provide validation or additional context.

session_start.description: Hooks that run when a session begins. Can load context or setup environment.

session_end.description: Hooks that run when a session ends. Can perform cleanup or logging.

on_user_input.description: Hooks that run when the agent needs user input. Can send notifications or log events.

stop.description: Hooks that run when the model finishes responding and is about to hand control back to the user. Can perform post-response validation or logging.

notification.description: Hooks that run when the agent sends a notification (error, warning) to the user. Can send external notifications or log events.

HookMatcherConfig.description: Configuration for matching tools and their associated hooks

matcher.description: Regex pattern to match tool names (e.g., 'shell|edit_file'). Use '*' to match all tools. Case-sensitive.

hooks.description: Hooks to execute when the matcher matches

HookDefinition.description: Definition of a single hook command

type.description: Type of hook (currently only 'command' is supported)

command.description: Shell command to execute. Receives JSON input via stdin with tool/session information.

base_url.description: Base URL for the model API

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel tool calls

provider_opts.description: Provider-specific options. dmr: runtime_flags. anthropic/amazon-bedrock (Claude): interleaved_thinking (boolean, default true). openai/anthropic/google: rerank_prompt (string) to fully override the system prompt used for RAG reranking (advanced - prefer using results.reranking.criteria for domain-specific guidance).

thinking_budget.description: Controls reasoning effort/budget. Use 'none' or 0 to disable thinking. OpenAI: string levels ('minimal','low','medium','high'). Anthropic: integer token budget (1024-32768), 'adaptive' (lets the model decide), or effort levels ('low','medium','high','max') which use adaptive thinking with the given effort. Amazon Bedrock (Claude): integer token budget or effort levels ('low','medium','high') mapped to token budgets. Google Gemini 2.5: integer token budget (-1 for dynamic, 0 to disable, 24576 max). Google Gemini 3: string levels ('minimal' Flash only,'low','medium','high'). Thinking is only enabled when explicitly configured.

oneOf.description: Reasoning effort level. 'adaptive'/'max' are Anthropic-specific. Use 'none' to disable thinking.
Token budget for extended thinking (Anthropic, Bedrock Claude, Gemini 2.5). Use 0 to disable thinking.

routing.description: Routing rules for request-based model selection. When configured, this model becomes a router that selects the best model based on the user's input. The model's provider/model fields define the fallback model.

RoutingRule.description: A single routing rule that maps example phrases to a target model

model.description: Model reference (another model name in the models section or inline spec like 'openai/gpt-4o')

examples.description: Example phrases that should trigger routing to this model

version.description: Version of the agent configuration (used for OCI registry publishing)

PermissionsConfig.description: Tool permission configuration. Controls tool call approval behavior with optional argument matching.

allow.description: Tool patterns that are auto-approved without user confirmation. Supports tool names with glob patterns (e.g., 'read_*') and argument matching (e.g., 'shell:cmd=ls*' to allow shell commands starting with 'ls'). MCP tools can use qualified names (e.g., 'mcp:github:*').

ask.description: Tool patterns that always require user confirmation, even for tools that are normally auto-approved (e.g. read-only tools). Supports the same pattern syntax as allow: tool names with globs and argument matching (e.g., 'fetch' to always ask before fetching URLs).

deny.description: Tool patterns that are always rejected. Takes priority over allow patterns. Supports the same pattern syntax as allow: tool names with globs and argument matching (e.g., 'shell:cmd=rm -rf*' to block dangerous rm commands).

AuditConfig.description: Audit trail configuration for governance and compliance. When enabled, all agent actions (tool calls, file operations, HTTP requests, etc.) are recorded with cryptographic signatures for tamper-proof audit trails.

storage_path.description: Directory to store audit records (default: data dir)

key_path.description: Path to the signing key file (default: storage_path/audit_key)

record_tool_calls.description: Record tool calls (default: true if enabled)

record_file_ops.description: Record file operations (default: true if enabled)

record_http.description: Record HTTP requests (default: true if enabled)

record_commands.description: Record command executions (default: true if enabled)

record_sessions.description: Record session start/end events (default: true if enabled)

include_input_content.description: Include user input content in audit records (default: false)

include_output_content.description: Include tool output content in audit records (default: false)

MCPToolset.description: Reusable MCP server definition. Define once at the top level and reference by name from agent toolsets.

command.description: Command to run the MCP server (stdio transport)

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

env.description: Environment variables for the MCP server

tools.description: Optional list of tools to expose from the MCP server

instruction.description:
Custom instruction for this MCP server's tools. By default, setting this field replaces the toolset's built-in instructions entirely. To enrich (rather than replace) the original instructions, include the placeholder {ORIGINAL_INSTRUCTIONS} in your text — it will be substituted with the toolset's built-in instructions at runtime. For example: '{ORIGINAL_INSTRUCTIONS}
Always prefer JSON output.' will prepend the original instructions and append your extra guidance.

name.description: Optional display name override for the MCP server

defer.description: Deferred loading configuration for tools from this MCP server

oneOf.description: Set to true to defer all tools
Array of tool names to defer

instruction.description:
Custom instruction for this toolset. By default, setting this field replaces the toolset's built-in instructions entirely. To enrich (rather than replace) the original instructions, include the placeholder {ORIGINAL_INSTRUCTIONS} in your text — it will be substituted with the toolset's built-in instructions at runtime. For example: '{ORIGINAL_INSTRUCTIONS}
Always prefer JSON output.' will prepend the original instructions and append your extra guidance.

toon.description: A comma-delimited list of regular expressions of tools to toonify

model.description: Model to use for the LLM turn that processes tool results from this toolset. Enables per-tool model routing: cheaper/faster models handle simple tool results (e.g. knowledge-base lookups, file reads) while the agent's primary model handles complex reasoning. Value can be a model name from the models section or an inline provider/model format (e.g. 'openai/gpt-4o-mini').

ref.description: Reference to a Docker MCP tool (e.g., 'docker:context7') or a named MCP definition from the top-level 'mcps' section

command.description: Command to execute for MCP tools

shared.description: Whether the tool is shared (for think tool)

ignore_vcs.description: Whether to ignore VCS files (.git directories and .gitignore patterns) in filesystem operations. Default: true

defer.description: Enable deferred loading for tools in this toolset. Set to true to defer all tools, or an array of tool names to defer only those tools. Deferred tools are not loaded into the agent's context immediately, but can be discovered and loaded on-demand using search_tool and add_tool.

oneOf.description: Set to true to defer all tools
Array of tool names to defer

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for the fetch tool

url.description: URL for the a2a or openapi tool

headers.description: HTTP headers for API requests (supports ${env.VAR} interpolation)

file_types.description: File extensions this LSP server handles (e.g., [".go", ".mod"]). Only for lsp toolsets.

models.description: List of allowed models for the model_picker tool.

version.description: Package reference for auto-installation of MCP/LSP tool binaries. Format: 'owner/repo' or 'owner/repo@version'. Set to 'false' to disable auto-install for this toolset.

transport_type.description: Transport type for the remote connection

oauth.description: Explicit OAuth configuration for remote MCP servers that do not support Dynamic Client Registration

RemoteOAuthConfig.description: Explicit OAuth configuration for remote MCP servers. Allows using pre-registered OAuth clients with servers that do not support Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591), such as the Slack MCP server.

client_id.description: OAuth client ID (required for explicit OAuth)

client_secret.description: OAuth client secret (optional, for confidential clients)

callback_port.description: Fixed port for the OAuth callback server (optional). When not specified, a random available port is used.

scopes.description: List of OAuth scopes to request (optional). When not specified, default scopes from the server are used.

args.description: Arguments schema (passed as properties in JSON schema)

path.description: Path pattern for files to apply post-edit command

ApiConfig.description: API tool configuration for making HTTP requests to external APIs

instruction.description: Instructions for using the API tool

args.description: Arguments schema for the API call

output_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the API tool's output. Used by MCP/Code Mode; tool responses are still returned as strings at runtime.

RAGConfig.description: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) configuration for document search and retrieval with pluggable strategies. Multiple strategies enable hybrid retrieval and reranking.

tool.description: Tool configuration for the RAG source

name.description: Custom name for the tool (defaults to RAG source name if not specified)

description.description: Description of what the tool does (shown to the LLM when selecting tools)

instruction.description: Instruction on how the RAG tool should be used effectively (shown in system prompt)

docs.description: Shared document paths or directories indexed by all strategies

respect_vcs.description: Whether to respect VCS ignore files (e.g., .gitignore) when collecting documents for indexing. When true (default), files matching ignore patterns will be excluded. Can be overridden per-strategy.

strategies.description: Array of retrieval strategy configurations. Each strategy can have different parameters based on its type.

items.description: Retrieval strategy configuration with type-specific parameters. Structured fields are limited; additional parameters are passed through as-is for strategy-specific use.

embedding_model.description: Embedding model reference for chunked-embeddings and semantic-embeddings strategies (looked up in models map, or 'auto' for automatic selection)

docs.description: Additional documents for this strategy only (augments shared docs)

database.description: Database path or connection string. Currently only simple string values are supported (e.g., './vector.db', './bm25.db').

similarity_metric.description: Similarity metric (chunked-embeddings only). Currently only 'cosine_similarity' is implemented.

vector_dimensions.description: Vector dimensions for embeddings (chunked-embeddings only). Must match your embedding model's output dimensions and is required for chunked-embeddings strategies.

k1.description: BM25 term frequency saturation (bm25 only, typically 1.2-2.0)

threshold.description: Minimum score threshold (0-1 for chunked-embeddings, unbounded for bm25)

limit.description: Max results from this strategy (candidates for fusion). If unset, defaults to 5 in the implementation.

size.description: Chunk size in characters. If unset, defaults to 1000 in the implementation.

overlap.description: Overlap between chunks in characters. If unset, defaults to 75 in the implementation.

respect_word_boundaries.description: When true, chunks will split on the nearest whitespace boundary instead of at the exact character limit, preventing words from being truncated.

code_aware.description: Enable code-aware chunking for source files. When true, the chunking strategy will prefer AST-based or language-aware processors when available (tree-sitter based), and fall back to plain text chunking for unsupported languages.

embedding_batch_size.description: Number of text chunks to send to the embedding API in a single request (chunked-embeddings/semantic-embeddings only)

max_embedding_concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent embedding batch API requests. For semantic-embeddings, also controls parallel LLM calls for generating chunk summaries.

max_indexing_concurrency.description: Maximum number of files to index in parallel during initialization

respect_vcs.description: Override the RAG-level respect_vcs setting for this strategy only.

chat_model.description: Chat model used to generate semantic representations for each chunk (semantic-embeddings only, required)

semantic_prompt.description: Custom prompt template for semantic LLM. Uses JavaScript template literal syntax with the following placeholders: ${path} (full source file path), ${basename} (base name of file), ${chunk_index} (numeric chunk index), ${content} (raw chunk content), ${ast_context} (AST metadata when ast_context is enabled). Only applicable to semantic-embeddings strategy.

ast_context.description: Include TreeSitter-derived AST metadata in the semantic prompt (semantic-embeddings only, requires chunking.code_aware for best results)

results.description: Result post-processing configuration (fusion, deduplication, limiting). If omitted, sensible defaults are applied in code.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (top K)

fusion.description: Configuration for combining results from multiple strategies. If omitted and multiple strategies are configured, Reciprocal Rank Fusion (rrf) with k=60 is used.

k.description: RRF smoothing parameter k (only for RRF strategy)

weights.description: Strategy weights for weighted fusion (strategy name -> weight)

reranking.description: Configuration for reranking results using a specialized reranking model. Reranking re-scores the retrieved results to improve relevance accuracy.

model.description: Model reference for reranking (can be inline like 'dmr/model-name' or a reference to a defined model)

top_k.description: Optional: only rerank top K results for efficiency. When unset or 0, defaults to the global results.limit (which itself defaults to 15).

threshold.description: Optional: minimum score threshold after reranking (filter results below this score)

criteria.description: Optional: domain-specific relevance criteria to guide scoring. This text is appended to the base reranking prompt to customize what 'relevance' means for your use case. Supported by OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini providers (not DMR native reranking).
examples: Prioritize recent information and practical examples over historical context
When scoring relevance, focus on code examples and implementation details

return_full_content.description: Return full document content instead of just the matched chunk. The full document is read directly from the file system.
